title: Earnings call transcript: QuantumScape Q1 2026 beats EPS expectations
text: QuantumScape reported Q1 2026 earnings with an EPS of -0.16, surpassing the forecast of -0.18 by 11.11%. The company's stock rose 4.87% in aftermarket trading, reflecting investor optimism. Management reiterated full-year 2026 adjusted EBITDA loss guidance of $250 million–$275 million and full-year capital expenditures guidance of $40 million–$60 million, along with the intention to increase customer billings year over year. Guidance shows adjusted EBITDA loss of $250M–$275M for 2026, flagging a long road to profitability, with heavy spending plans focused on cash burn and balance sheet stamina. Analysts consider the stock a Hold, citing potential opportunities due to Volkswagen's strong position in the EV market. Customer billings of $11.0 million in Q1 compared with roughly $19.5 million for the entire prior fiscal year, with executives reiterating expectations for higher billings year over year in 2026.
